title: COVID-19 Commemoration Programme: List of COVID-19 memorials
body: "  To find a COVID-19 memorial, you can:   view an interactive map browse by region and view a list    The map and lists are updated regularly as new memorials are added.  Please note, memorial descriptions and details have been written and submitted by the public or the custodians of the memorial, and may include a mix of street addresses and What3Word locations. The UK government is not responsible for any memorials listed nor for any errors in the information provided.  East Midlands      Local authority: Broxtowe Borough Council  Memorial: COVID-19 Memorial Trees     Description: A group of 30 Silver Birch trees planted as a living memorial to COVID-19, two meters apart symbolising social distancing during the pandemic, and underplanted with bluebells to reflect the NHS colours.  Location: Bramcote Hills Park, Ilkeston Rd, Bramcote, Nottinghamshire NG9 3JT       Local authority: Charnwood Borough Council  Memorial: Hope Bell      Description: A seven and a half metre tall bell installation to commemorate the lives lost during the COVID-19 pandemic. The Hope Bell symbolises unity, remembrance, and hope for the future.  Location: Queen’s Park, Loughborough LE11 3BJ       Local authority: Melton Borough Council  Memorial: Circle of Life - COVID Memorial Garden      Description: A reflective garden in Melton Country Park featuring seasonal plantings, benches, and a central sculpture honouring frontline workers and those affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.  Location: Willow Drive Entrance, Melton Mowbray LE13 (What3Words: bath.stand.hiding)   East of England      Local authority: Chelmsford Council  Memorial: Springfield Oaks and Memorial Plaque      Description: 24 oak trees planted on Springfield Green, an avenue of native woodland and wildflower meadows, one for each ward of Chelmsford in memory of the lives affected by COVID-19.  Location: pits.roof.fuels       Local authority: Colchester City Council  Memorial: COVID-19 Memorial      Description: A memorial stone located next to the Colchester Crematorium Chapel, serving as a COVID-19 place of reflection.  Location: Colchester Crematorium Chapel, Mersea Road, Colchester CO2 8RU (What3Words: fills.quarrel.guises)       Local authority: Colchester City Council  Memorial: COVID-19 Memorial Bench and Stone      Description: A memorial bench and stone surrounded by reflecting planting, situated at Silver Witch Green to honour the lives lost to COVID-19.  Location: Silver Witch Green, Colchester, CO3 0PS (What3Words: inch.breath.foods)       Local authority: Colchester City Council  Memorial: COVID-19 Blossom Circles     Description: Four blossom circles planted at Kings Meadow in Castle Park, Colchester as a place of reflection to remember the lives lost to COVID-19.  Location: effort.finely.pines   London      Local authority: Brent Council  Memorial: COVID Memorial Tree     Description: A tree planted opposite the aviary in Roundwood Park (closest entrance to the park is Harlesden Road), in memorial of the lives lost during the COVID-19 pandemic.  Location: Roundwood Park, Harlesden Road, London NW10 2SH       Local authority: City of London  Memorial: Remember Me Memorial      Description: An online memorial to those who died as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic in the UK. It is free to leave a tribute to a loved one. The memorial is open to people of all faiths and one, and will remain open for entries for as long as needed.  Location: St. Paul’s Churchyard, London EC4M 8AD       Local authority: Enfield...
